title: Project Manifest
description: Per-folder .project.json — source of truth for the C:/dev repos manifest. Place at the root of every project or group folder. The sync script (portfolio/scripts/sync-projects.ts) reads these and emits C:/dev/.projects.json.

$schema.description: Path/URL to this schema for IDE autocomplete.

id.description: Stable, URL-safe identifier. Should not change once set.

kind.description: project = a real repo/codebase. group = a folder containing other projects/subgroups (e.g. Games).

type.description: What kind of thing this is. Required for projects; optional for groups.

status.description: Lifecycle stage. Active = under development. Maintained = shipped, occasional updates. Experimental = prototype/spike. Archived = frozen.

isCodeRepo.description: True for code repos; false for content/knowledge folders (e.g. Obsidian vault). If omitted, sync auto-derives from .git presence.

deployment.description: Where this project lives or deploys. Array — a project can have multiple (e.g. github + vercel). If omitted, sync auto-derives from git remote and config files.

group.description: ID of the parent group, or null/omitted if top-level. Must reference an existing group manifest.

description.description: One-line description. Keep short — long-form lives in README, VISION.md, or portfolio.json.

tags.description: Cross-cutting labels (e.g. 'revenue-eligible', 'wip', 'public', 'showcase'). Do not duplicate values covered by type/kind/status.
